explanation: The requirement is that the system must measure and display the breathing rate. We have two atomic propositions: 'measureRR' (indicates the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (indicates the system displays the breathing rate). To satisfy the requirement, we must ensure that the system always (globally) measures the breathing rate and displays it. Thus, we represent this as both 'measureRR' and 'displayRR' always being true. The template '(G bool_exp3)' is suitable, where bool_exp3 = '(measureRR & displayRR)'.

The requirement is that the system measures and displays the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). In the provided atomic propositions, 'measureRR' indicates that the system measures the breathing rate, and 'displayRR' indicates that the system displays the breathing rate. To fully satisfy the requirement, both measuring and displaying the breathing rate need to be true. Therefore, a suitable translation is to specify that it is always the case (globally) that both are true: G (measureRR & displayRR). This asserts that at all times, the system is both measuring and displaying the breathing rate, capturing the intent of the requirement as a continuous guarantee.

The natural language states that the system must continuously provide two capabilities: measuring the breathing rate and displaying the breathing rate to the user. These are expressed by the atomic propositions 'measureRR' (measuring breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (displaying rate). The requirement suggests that both functionalities should be always provided, meaning at all times during the system's operation. In LTL, this is best represented by globally (G) enforcing both propositions to hold. Therefore, the property 'G (measureRR & displayRR)' guarantees that, at every point in the execution, the system measures and displays the breathing rate, fulfilling the natural language requirement.

The requirement specifies that the system shall both measure and display the breathing rate. Mapping to atomic propositions, 'measureRR' is for measurement and 'displayRR' is for displaying the breathing rate. The strongest and most accurate guarantee is to ensure that, globally, if the system measures the breathing rate, it must also display it. The template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' fits this, setting bool_exp1 = measureRR (measuring happens) and bool_exp3 = displayRR (displaying must follow each measurement). This ensures at all times, whenever measuring the rate occurs, it is accompanied by displaying it—matching the input requirement for both measurement and display. Other possible templates (like 'G bool_exp3') would require always displaying regardless of measurement, which is not quite as direct. Some AND combinations would be overly strict or not logical given atomic prop granularity.

The requirement states that the system shall measure and display the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). The atomic propositions available are 'measureRR' (indicates the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (indicates the system displays the breathing rate). The most direct translation is to guarantee that always (globally) both measuring and displaying the breathing rate are true. This can be represented as a conjunction '(measureRR & displayRR)' that should always hold, which means: at every state, the system is both measuring and displaying the breathing rate. This captures the asserted requirement without implying temporal ordering between these actions, which matches the natural language implication that both conditions must always be true in the operational context.

The natural language requirement states the system shall both measure and display the breathing rate. There are atomic propositions that map directly to these actions: 'measureRR' (indicates the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (indicates the system displays the breathing rate). To fulfill the requirement, we want to express that it is always the case (globally) that measuring the respiratory rate implies that the rate is displayed. The most suitable LTL template is G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where 'bool_exp1' is 'measureRR' and 'bool_exp3' is 'displayRR'. This captures the requirement that whenever the system measures the breathing rate, it must also display it.

The requirement states that the system should both measure and display the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). The atomic propositions provided contain 'measureRR' (indicates the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (indicates the system displays the breathing rate) which directly align with this requirement. The best LTL template is to specify that 'always, if (measureRR) then (displayRR)' (i.e., whenever the breathing rate is measured, it is also displayed). This ensures the system is always displaying the breathing rate whenever it is being measured. Template 30 was chosen: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 = measureRR and bool_exp3 = displayRR. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The natural language states the system shall both measure and display the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). The atomic propositions relevant here are 'measureRR' (the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (the system displays the breathing rate). To capture the requirement that both these actions should always occur as required, we use 'G (measureRR & displayRR)'. This expresses that at all times, both measuring and displaying the breathing rate are true, capturing the intent that the system always measures and displays the breathing rate. If only one of these is expected at a time, a disjunction (|) could be used. Here, the emphasis from the requirement is on both actions occurring throughout system operation.

The requirement states that the system must measure and display the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). This is best captured by the LTL property that says it is always true (globally) that if the system measures the breathing rate, it will also display the breathing rate. This is done by expressing an implication: whenever 'measureRR' is true, then 'displayRR' must also be true at the same time. This ensures that every time a breathing rate measurement is taken, it is always displayed. The chosen template (#30) directly captures this causal relationship using the available atomic propositions and their descriptions.

The requirement is that the system must measure and display the breathing rate (number of breaths per minute). Among the available atomic propositions, 'measureRR' indicates the system measures the breathing rate, and 'displayRR' indicates the system displays the breathing rate. Since both measurement and display are required, we express this as 'measureRR & displayRR'. The appropriate LTL template is 'G bool_exp3', which asserts that this condition holds globally, i.e., at all times the system should be measuring and displaying the breathing rate.
